name: Saffron Rice
description: The Mediterranean Dish's saffron rice — basmati toasted with onion and garlic, simmered in saffron-bloomed broth with a bay leaf and bright citrus zest. A 30-minute, golden, fragrant upgrade over plain white rice.
recipeIngredient: 10 saffron strands, ground, or 1/8 teaspoon ground saffron
1 small yellow or white onion, finely diced
1 1/2 cups vegetable broth, chicken broth, or water
recipeInstructions: Bloom the saffron: grind the threads to a powder, then mix 1/8 teaspoon with 3 tablespoons water and set aside.
Rinse the rice in a fine-mesh strainer under cold water until the water runs clear.
Heat the olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium-high. Add the onion and cook until translucent, then add the garlic and stir ~30 seconds.
Add the rice and toss to coat. Pour in the bloomed saffron liquid and mix thoroughly.
Add the broth, bay leaf, and lime zest; season with salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce to low, cover, and cook until the liquid is absorbed, 15-20 minutes.
Off the heat, rest covered 5 minutes. Remove the bay leaf, fluff with a fork, and serve.
author: The Mediterranean Dish

ai.cliffNotes: Bloom saffron, toast rinsed basmati with onion and garlic, simmer in saffron broth with bay and lime zest, rest, and fluff. Golden, fragrant, 30 minutes.
keyTips: Bloom the saffron (grind it, steep in a little water) before it goes in — it draws out far more color and flavor than tossing in whole threads.
Rinse the basmati until the water runs clear; washing off the surface starch is what gives you fluffy, separate grains instead of a clump.
Stick to the 1 cup rice : 1 1/2 cups liquid ratio for the right texture.
Let it rest covered 5 minutes off the heat before fluffing — the carryover steam finishes the grains evenly.
ai.source: Claude (prose-mined from The Mediterranean Dish article)
